title: Adina aralioides
taxonomy: Adina aralioides is classified under the family Rubiaceae, which is a large and diverse family of flowering plants. This plant belongs to the genus Adina, which is a group of flowering plants native to tropical Asia. The species Adina aralioides is further classified into the subfamily Ixoroideae. The classification of Adina aralioides is as follows: kingdom Plantae, phylum Angiospermophyta, class Magnoliopsida, order Gentianales, family Rubiaceae, genus Adina, and species A. aralioides. This classification helps in understanding the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of the plant.
uses: Adina aralioides is a plant native to the tropical forests of India and Southeast Asia. Traditionally, it has been used in various folk medicines to treat various health conditions. Adina aralioides is also used to treat fever, rheumatism, and other ailments. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal potential of this plant.
